feature.name: Trinity Build — Brain + Marketplace + Unified Inbox (Full)
feature.description: Full Trinity Build: security foundations, Brain page (memory feed, deal memory, contact memory, commitments, agent log, settings), Marketplace (DB-backed abilities, fleet/orchestrator/cron gating, run tracking), Unified Inbox (left-rail refactor, context injection, inline actions, chain replay, resolution tracking).

stories.title: Fix merge conflicts in 3 memory migration files
acceptance: supabase/migrations/20260225100001_deal_memory_events.sql has no <<<<<<< markers
supabase/migrations/20260225100002_deal_memory_snapshots.sql has no <<<<<<< markers
supabase/migrations/20260225100005_contact_rep_memory.sql has no <<<<<<< markers
Use HEAD pattern (DO $$ BEGIN ... EXCEPTION ... END $$) for idempotent RLS policies
npx supabase db push --linked --dry-run passes without errors

stories.title: Fix copilot_memories RLS — add org scoping via clerk_org_id
acceptance: New migration drops existing user-only RLS policy on copilot_memories
New RLS policy checks auth.uid() = user_id AND org scoping via clerk_org_id matching user's active org
Multi-org users only see memories for their currently active org
Migration uses DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE POLICY pattern
Tested: user in org A cannot see memories with clerk_org_id of org B

stories.title: Add retention policy + decay_score index on copilot_memories
acceptance: New migration adds decay_score NUMERIC(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0 column to copilot_memories
Index on (user_id, decay_score DESC) WHERE decay_score > 0.1 created
pg_cron job created to delete memories older than 365 days with decay_score < 0.1
Migration is re-runnable (IF NOT EXISTS / DO blocks)

stories.title: Brain page scaffold — route, layout, 5 section tabs
acceptance: New route /agent/brain registered in App.tsx with lazy import
Sidebar link added under Agent section in AppLayout
BrainPage.tsx created following CommandCentre.tsx scaffold pattern
5 tabs: Memory Feed, Deal Memory, Contact Memory, Agent Log, Settings
Each tab renders a placeholder component with empty state
Page gated behind authenticated user (not admin-only — users see their own memories)

stories.title: Memory Feed tab — realtime stream of copilot_memories by category
acceptance: BrainMemoryFeed.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
useBrainMemories hook created — queries copilot_memories filtered by user_id + org
Supabase Realtime subscription on copilot_memories for live updates (table already published)
Filter by category: deal, contact, rep, commitment, general
Each memory card shows: category badge, subject, content preview, decay_score bar, last_accessed_at
Pagination with cursor-based loading (newest first)
Empty state when no memories exist

stories.title: Deal Memory viewer — grouped events with commitment timeline
acceptance: BrainDealMemory.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
Deal selector dropdown showing user's deals
Timeline view of deal_memory_events grouped by event_category
18 event categories rendered with distinct badges
Commitment events highlighted with due date and status (pending/fulfilled/broken)
Confidence score shown per event
Empty state when no deal selected or no events exist

stories.title: Wire cron-triggered abilities to respect user preferences before invocation
acceptance: proactive-meeting-prep/index.ts checks proactive_agent_config.is_enabled + user_sequence_preferences before generating prep
agent-deal-risk-batch/index.ts checks org config + user preferences before scoring
Stale deal revival cron handler checks preferences before processing
Campaign daily check cron handler checks preferences before pulling metrics
Coaching weekly cron handler checks preferences before aggregating
Users who toggled ability OFF in marketplace no longer receive cron-triggered notifications
Org-level master toggle (proactive_agent_config.is_enabled = false) skips all cron processing for that org

stories.title: Persist delivery channel preferences to DB instead of localStorage
acceptance: user_sequence_preferences.delivery_channel column used for channel prefs (already exists in schema)
MarketplaceAbilityCard + AbilityDetailSheet save channel toggles to DB via update_user_sequence_preference RPC
Remove localStorage reads/writes for delivery channels (agent-ability-channels-{id} keys)
Channel preferences survive across browsers/devices
Orchestrator runner reads delivery_channel from preferences and routes output accordingly
Default channels from abilityRegistry used when no DB preference exists

stories.title: Add run tracking — update last_run_at + run_count after each ability execution
acceptance: New migration adds last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ and run_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0 to user_sequence_preferences if not present
Orchestrator runner.ts updates last_run_at and increments run_count on sequence completion (success or failure)
Marketplace ability cards show last_run_at as relative time ('2h ago') and run_count badge
AbilityDetailSheet shows full run history summary from sequence_jobs table
Stats refresh on marketplace page load (no polling needed)

stories.title: Add resolved_via + thread_id columns to command_centre_items
acceptance: New migration adds resolved_via TEXT (copilot, manual, auto, slack)
Adds resolved_conversation_id UUID nullable
Adds thread_id UUID nullable for grouping related items
Index on (thread_id) WHERE thread_id IS NOT NULL
commandCentreItemsService.ts updated to set resolved_via on approval/dismiss/auto-resolve
Migration is re-runnable

stories.title: Add seedContext to useCopilotChat for CC item context injection
acceptance: SendMessageOptions in useCopilotChat.ts gains seedContext?: { itemId: string, itemType: string, dealId?: string, contactId?: string }
When seedContext provided, it's included in the API request body to copilot-autonomous
copilot-autonomous edge function accepts seed_context in request body and injects into system prompt
CC item detail panel gains 'Ask Copilot' button that opens copilot with seedContext pre-loaded
Existing copilot behavior unchanged when seedContext not provided

stories.title: Contact Memory tab — interaction graph with decay strength bars
acceptance: BrainContactMemory.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
Contact selector with search — shows contacts the user has interacted with
Per-contact view: relationship_strength bar (0-1 scale, color-coded green/yellow/red)
Decay visualization: shows strength over time using last_accessed_at and decay_score
Interaction timeline: meetings, emails, notes related to this contact from copilot_memories
Stale relationship warning when decay_score < 0.3
Empty state when no contact selected

stories.title: Commitments tracker tab — deadlines grouped by deal/contact
acceptance: BrainCommitments.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
Queries deal_memory_events WHERE event_category IN ('commitment_made', 'commitment_fulfilled', 'commitment_broken')
Grouped by: overdue (red), due this week (amber), upcoming (default), fulfilled (green, collapsed)
Each commitment shows: description, deal name, contact name, due date, status badge
Sort: overdue first, then by due date ascending
Broken commitments show risk_flag event if auto-generated
Mark as fulfilled button updates event via markCommitmentFulfilled() from commitments.ts

stories.title: Agent Log viewer tab — daily action log with chain grouping
acceptance: BrainAgentLog.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
Queries agent_daily_logs for user's org, ordered by created_at DESC
Filter by agent_type: morning-brief, post-meeting, deal-risk, etc.
Filter by outcome: success, failed, pending, cancelled, skipped
Chain grouping: items with same chain_id shown together with wave_number ordering
Each entry shows: agent_type badge, action_type, decision_reasoning preview, credit_cost, execution_ms
Expandable row reveals full action_detail JSONB and input_context_summary
Pagination: 50 entries per page, cursor-based

stories.title: Memory Settings tab — decay overrides, category purge, retention
acceptance: BrainSettings.tsx component created in src/components/brain/
Category toggles: enable/disable memory collection per category (deal, contact, rep, commitment, general)
Decay rate override: slider per category to adjust decay speed (0.5x slower to 2x faster)
Bulk purge: delete all memories for a specific deal, contact, or date range with confirmation dialog
Memory stats: total count per category, oldest/newest memory dates
Settings persisted to user_settings JSONB column (memory_preferences key)
Changes take effect on next memory write/decay cycle

stories.title: Unified Inbox — left-rail layout refactor with item list + detail panel
acceptance: CommandCentre.tsx refactored from vertical feed to left-rail/right-panel layout
Left rail (w-[380px]): scrollable item list with existing CCItemCard components
Right panel (flex-1): selected item detail with CCDetailPanel + copilot chat area
Item selection highlights in left rail, loads detail on right
Keyboard navigation preserved: j/k moves selection, Enter opens detail
Deep links preserved: /command-centre?item=uuid selects that item
Responsive: on mobile (<768px), falls back to single-column with back navigation
Existing filter bar, grouping, and bulk actions remain functional

stories.title: Inline actions — reply email, create task, update CRM from CC item
acceptance: CCDetailPanel gains inline action buttons beyond approve/dismiss/snooze
Reply Email: opens email composer pre-filled with CC item context (contact, deal, suggested content)
Create Task: opens QuickAdd task modal with title/description pre-filled from CC item
Update CRM: for deal_risk items, offers one-click stage change or field update via existing deal service
Each action sets resolved_via = 'manual' on the CC item after completion
Actions respect autonomy tier: suggest-tier items show as suggestions, approve-tier items show Approve button
Error toast on action failure, success toast on completion

stories.title: Chain replay — surface agent_daily_logs reasoning chain for CC items
acceptance: CCChainReplay.tsx component created in src/components/commandCentre/
When a CC item has source_agent set, queries agent_daily_logs for matching chain_id
Renders chain as a step-by-step timeline: wave 1 → wave 2 → ... → final output
Each step shows: agent_type, action_type, decision_reasoning, outcome badge, credit_cost
Collapsible by default, expandable via 'Show reasoning' link in CCDetailPanel
If no chain_id found, shows 'Direct action — no reasoning chain available'
Handles loading state and errors gracefully

stories.title: Bulk actions — multi-select approve/dismiss/snooze from left rail
acceptance: Checkbox added to each CCItemCard for multi-select
Shift+click selects range (standard pattern)
Bulk action bar appears when 2+ items selected: Approve All, Dismiss All, Snooze All
Bulk approve calls existing mutation for each selected item (batched, max 25)
Confirmation dialog for bulk dismiss: 'Dismiss N items? This cannot be undone.'
Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+A selects all visible items
Selection count badge shown in bulk action bar
All mutations set resolved_via = 'manual'

parallelGroups.reason: All independent foundation fixes — merge conflicts, RLS, retention
Brain scaffold, cron preference wiring, CC schema — all independent after Group 1
Brain memory feed + deal memory + delivery channel DB persistence
Run tracking, copilot seedContext, contact memory, commitments
Agent log viewer, memory settings, left-rail inbox refactor
Inline actions, chain replay, bulk actions — all need left-rail done

deferredToV2: Autonomy tier selector in Marketplace UI (currently just enable/disable toggle)
Credit budget 80% throttle warning (currently hard cap at 100% only)
Real autonomy gate implementation (currently stub returning { allowed: true })
Copilot memory recall in seedContext (fetch relevant memories before API call)
Memory decay background job (cron to refresh decay_score periodically)
Rep Memory tab in Brain page (learned preferences, tone calibration)
V1 abilities migration from localStorage to DB-backed preferences
